PROCEDURE NOTES — GASTROENTEROLOGY
Patient: Harris, Dorothy | DOB: 08/11/1952 | MRN: MRN89012765
Date of Procedure: 10/22/2025
Physician: Dr. George Martin, MD — Gastroenterology Specialists

PROCEDURE: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) with biopsy

INDICATIONS:
1. K21.0 — Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) with esophagitis
2. K44.9 — Diaphragmatic hernia without obstruction or gangrene
3. K57.30 — Diverticulosis of large intestine without perforation or abscess

HISTORY:
Ms. Harris is a 72-year-old female with a long history of GERD refractory to maximum-dose PPI therapy (omeprazole 40mg BID x 12 weeks). She reports persistent heartburn, regurgitation, dysphagia to solids, and unintentional weight loss of 8 lbs over 3 months. She also has a known diaphragmatic hernia. Prior barium swallow (09/2025) showed a 4cm sliding hiatal hernia with reflux. Given her age, alarm symptoms (dysphagia, weight loss), and refractory GERD, diagnostic EGD was indicated per ACG guidelines.

PROCEDURES PERFORMED:
1. CPT 43235 — EGD, diagnostic, upper GI endoscopy
2. CPT 43239 — EGD with biopsy, single or multiple
3. J1100 — Injection, dexamethasone sodium phosphate, 1 mg (administered for laryngeal edema prophylaxis)

FINDINGS:
- Esophagus: Grade C esophagitis (Los Angeles classification) wi

args.text: Triage review DEN-016 / CLM-2025-00016: Anthem Blue Cross Medicare Advantage denied DOS 10/22/2025 as CO-50 medical necessity (remarks N657/N386), balance $8,750, appeal deadline 03/12/2026. Services billed include EGD diagnostic CPT 43235, EGD with biopsy CPT 43239, and J1100 by Dr. George Martin; dx include K21.0 GERD with esophagitis, K44.9 diaphragmatic hernia, and K57.30. Procedure note supports medical necessity: 72 y/o with long-standing GERD refractory to maximum-dose PPI therapy (omeprazole 40 mg BID x12 weeks), persistent heartburn/regurgitation, dysphagia to solids, unintentional 8-lb weight loss over 3 months, known hiatal hernia, and 09/2025 barium swallow showing 4 cm sliding hiatal hernia with reflux. Note states EGD was indicated per ACG guidelines due to age, alarm symptoms, and refractory GERD, with finding of Grade C esophagitis. No coding/eligibility/timely filing issue identified from available information. Disposition: Route to Clinical Appeals for medical-necessity appeal with procedure note/guideline support before deadline.

args.text: CO-50 medical necessity denial for Anthem MA claim CLM-2025-00016, DOS 10/22/2025, balance $8,750. EGD 43235/43239 with J1100 for GERD with esophagitis/hiatal hernia. Procedure note supports necessity: age 72, refractory GERD despite omeprazole 40mg BID x12 weeks, dysphagia, 8-lb weight loss, prior barium swallow with 4cm sliding hiatal hernia/reflux, Grade C esophagitis. No coding, eligibility, or timely filing issue seen. Route to Clinical Appeals for medical necessity appeal before 03/12/2026 deadline.
